message: Hey everyone. I'm pumped today about our flash sale
You know it's been 56 years since the Beatles released Abbey Road right? My favourite album
56 years already?? Wow that makes me feel old...
Indeed! We're celebrating today by promoting a flash sale on retro items from 1969

Welcome to Bezs Beatles Day, remember communication is key to diagnose the problem.

 *Access game links by pressing the Uptime Labs Logo in the top right of the screen*
 *Use `/incident` to create an incident record*

Once you're ready just click start to begin
*Incident Number INC100086:* P1 incident - Checkout down
We're a step closer to allowing folks to pay for items from the 1960s with Bitcoin thanks to Daniel!
Think of it like time travelling!
Wow good work Daniel! Looking forward to seeing it in action in prod
How's the test-driven development going?
TDDs going well. We are as solid as a rock! Though don't ask me what the Liskov Substitution Principle is...
erm - funny you should say that Daniel, I'm just testing my checkout service change in prod and I can't complete the payment - something's up
I hope my checkout service change is all good. Can someone check its not just me? And before you ask, yes I've cleared my cookies
Sorry Daniel it's not looking good on my end either
Uh oh...here we go again
@kumar.gyanendra-zomato.com can you "Size up the Issue" please and keep us informed
From what I can see it looks like we are still getting orders through
@shay checking
I just tried to place an order and my payment didn't go through either.
Get this sorted ASAP!!!
Yes there is an issue
I'm seeing a spike in failed transactions—about 40% of customer payments are not going through right now.
*Incident Number INC100093:* P1 checkout down
Acknowledged. What is the current impact in terms of affected customers and lost orders? Also, which regions are experiencing this outage?
I tried making a payment myself and it failed as well, so I'm seeing the same problem.
@hamed can you please check
Just to update, the failure rate is still around 40% for customer payments, and we're continuing to see a high number of unsuccessful transactions.
Iam also checking the flow
:star: Reproduces the incident
Let me know if you need any specific metrics or details about the failed transactions as you check the flow.
We have an incident. Will update as we make investigation

@channel 

below is the error :

#### Payment Unavailable

Sorry for the inconvenience, but we're currently unable to process your payment right now.

* * *

Please note that you will not be charged until the issue is resolved.

Our engineers are actively working to restoring service.

We recommend trying again later, or selecting a different payment method if available.

[Back to shop](https://hipster.prod.uptimelabs.io/)
:star: Qualify the problem
Thanks for sharing the error message—this matches what we're seeing in our monitoring, with a significant spike in payment failures affecting about 40% of transactions.
Here are the recent deployments : https://uptime-labs.atlassian.net/wiki/external/OGU1YjE2MmE4ZmI4NGFkMjgzNzEwZGVkYjlmNzgwNWY
Understood. I need specifics: how many customers are impacted, and which regions are affected? Are we currently losing revenue?
@daniel @hamed Please get on the call
I'm joining the call now and will keep you updated as I investigate the payment failures.
thx
Let me know if you need updated metrics or more details on the affected transactions as you coordinate.
Order rate is down from 60 to 20
Team is on it
@daniel did you find anything. Do you need any help
:star: Manage actions and timings
I'm still looking into the issue and will let you know if I need any assistance.
That's a significant drop. Which regions are seeing this decline? Keep me posted on progress and ETA for resolution.
@tanya Can you join
I'm joining the call now and ready to help however I can.

Its worth checking recent deployments as i can see there are few changes related to payments here 
https://uptime-labs.atlassian.net/wiki/external/OGU1YjE2MmE4ZmI4NGFkMjgzNzEwZGVkYjlmNzgwNWY
please revert the changes

Give me a second, I'll get the detailed info on each of the changes made today
Hope this helps: https://uptime-labs.atlassian.net/wiki/external/OGU1YjE2MmE4ZmI4NGFkMjgzNzEwZGVkYjlmNzgwNWY
Please revert the change first
Any updates on the investigation?
I'll start rolling back the PaymentDatabase (CHG89350) and AdService (CHG89359) changes now.

I see this change was made by Daniel 
Change ID: CHG89362

Owner: Daniel

Service: Payment Service

Environment: PROD

Version Deployed: v1.8.0

Time Of Change: 20 minutes ago

Description: Incremental feature release and bug fix to improve card payment reliability
@daniel please revert
Daniel must be able to answer more about his change
My code isn't even being called yet, so I don't think rolling back is necessary.
We will check later. Please revert first
Would make more sense to investigate Shay's change first?
Daniel has a point - let's try Shay's change first before touching the payment service
Its Europe
It seems we have identified the service
Understood, I'll revert my PaymentService change (CHG89362) now.
Will update
Thank you. Confirm the number of affected customers in Europe and provide an estimated time to resolution. Are we able to recover any lost orders?
Do update on the progress
I've started the rollback of my PaymentService change (CHG89362) and will confirm once it's complete.
What is the progress @daniel
The rollback of my PaymentService change (CHG89362) is complete and the previous version is now live.
Noted. Keep me informed as soon as you have confirmation on the fix and impact to revenue.
Let us check the flow now
I'm still seeing a 100% payment failure rate with no successful transactions since the rollback.
I still see the error
I will start checking the logs now
Anyone else has any idea
Could it be your checkout change again @Shay? It broke the platform last week!
The checkout service logs are healthy and the service is working as intended
I see 504 HTTP errors in the payment service, which means the application is failing to serve the requests
@shay any deployment in the service
:star: Check changes done to the platform
504 errors usually indicate that the payment service is timing out while waiting for a response from an upstream dependency.
Yes, I deployed CheckoutService version 1.5.5 to production about 1 hour ago as part of CHG89360.
@shay Please revert
I will rollback my checkout change right away
Rollback complete!
Hmm, customer reports are still coming in at the same rate
The failure pattern hasn't changed - still 100% failure in the UK
We are doing the second revert
Something else must be causing this
Fingers crossed
We need this fixed!
Understood. Once the revert is complete, confirm immediately if order flow and revenue are restored.
Just got the reports again. Users can do checkout
Folks , please check all logs and anything wrong anywhere
I've been digging into the recent changes and noticed that Daniel modified some shared libraries used specifically in the UK (GBP) payment flows, which could be related to the issue.
I'll keep reviewing the checkout logs, but so far nothing unusual is showing up on my end.
I can see order metrics are going up
checking @bob
I'm still seeing a 100% payment failure rate in the UK, with no improvement after the recent rollbacks.
@bob Please check again. Checkout seems to be working
:star: Cross check resolution with stakeholders
Unfortunately, I'm still seeing zero successful payments in the UK and the issue persists.
I need certainty, not hope. Notify me the moment you have confirmation that orders and revenue are back to normal.
Daniel are you sure you've rolled back your payments change ?
@bob where are you seeing these errors?
I'm seeing these errors in our customer order dashboard and payment failure logs, all specific to UK transactions.
I can do a successful checkout
:star: Validate the fix
That's strange, because my metrics still show a 100% failure rate for UK payments with no successful transactions recorded.
Please can you validate the checkout yourself. Its working for me
:star: Cross checking resolution with team
I just attempted a test checkout using a UK payment method and it failed at the payment step, matching what we're seeing in the logs.
@daniel Can you check again.
I double-checked my code and tests, and everything still looks solid on my end, but I'm open to digging deeper if needed.
Was the payment revert complete
Yes, my checkout service rollback was completed and confirmed earlier.
Do we need to revert anything else
Based on my investigation, Daniel's recent change to shared libraries used in UK payment flows could be worth reverting to see if it resolves the issue.
We are still debugging
Understood, but I need a clear ETA for resolution. Are we continuing to lose orders and revenue during this period? Quantify the ongoing impact.
Oh, sorry , I was looking at other reports. My team just updated me on successful checkouts
Its definitely working again
@daniel can you try reverting these changes too
The new code isn't being invoked yet, can I investigate a bit more.
I don't think the Beatles would've anticipated cryptocurrency so let's keep it that way for now

We seem to be back online
:star: Confirm resolution with the business
@bob do you have the correct link. Its working for me and the checkout matrices are also up

Confirm immediately: are orders processing at normal rates and is revenue flowing as expected? I need concrete numbers.
@channel can everyone else try again once
I also attempted a UK payment and it failed, so the issue is still present for me as well.
I just tried a UK checkout and I'm still getting a payment failure at the final step.
We are confirming. Matrices are up but We are still in discussion with team
Is there a specific flow where its happening?
All the failures I'm seeing are specific to UK payment flows, regardless of product or cart size.
What flow you guys are trying
I'm testing the standard GBP payment flow with a UK shipping address and it's failing at the payment authorization step.
I need a definitive answer on revenue and order flow—no assumptions. Update me as soon as you have verified data.
I am not seeing that errror. @daniel anyways revert the payment changes @tanya was talking about
Fine if you insist, I'll rollback
Rolling back my Bitcoin payment service change now

@bob please check

Customer payments appear to be back up and processing normally
@channel others please check

Logs are looking clean, no more 504 errors.
My test payment went through successfully! Looks like we're back in business.
cool
Excellent! Get me a full incident report by end of day
Great work everyone - crisis averted!
We are definitely back up again
Good. Confirm the total number of affected customers and quantify any lost revenue during the outage. I need a summary report ASAP.
We will open an RCA
Yes we will open an RCA
